reviewer.findings: C2 wires ring_mul (NTT fast path + debug-only schoolbook cross-check) into the 4 live poly_mul call sites in keygen_internal_prod, kpke_encrypt (x2), kpke_decrypt. No scheme change: t/s/u/v remain coefficient-domain, only the ring-multiply implementation changed. kem_golden_vectors_frozen (added in C1, captured pre-wire) still passes byte-for-byte after this swap, and all 12 pq_kem tests pass (cargo test -p bebop2-core --lib pq_kem::), confirming behaviour-neutrality. debug_assert_eq! in ring_mul keeps poly_mul as a live, permanent oracle rather than dead code in debug/test builds; it compiles out in release.

overlap.findings: Cross-checked against the exhaustive proof already on main (ntt_kem_exhaustive_basis_proof: all 65536 monomial basis pairs, poly_mul_ntt == poly_mul) which is the substantive correctness authority for this swap — C2 only wires that already-proven equivalence into the live path, it does not introduce new cryptographic claims. Verified the swap touches exactly the 4 call sites named in the plan and no others (grep confirms the one remaining poly_mul(&a[i][j], &s[j]) call is inside a test-only reference reimplementation in mod tests, correctly left untouched). No production behavior change beyond speed.
